pattern: Collapse each customer down to a single number — their total across every account — and keep only the biggest such total.
hints: Each row is one customer; you don't need to track individual accounts once you know a row's total.
Track a running maximum across the row sums instead of collecting all of them first.

edge_inputs.description: a customer with many small accounts beats a customer with few large ones only if the sum is actually bigger
single row spanning the max column count
single column spanning the max row count
constraint-max shape and values: 50 customers by 50 accounts, all at value 100
the richest customer is not the first row

stress.description: random rectangular grid of account balances at varying customer/account counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 50)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 50)))
    accounts = [[rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [accounts]
stress.note: random m x n grid within the constraint caps
